title: ComplianceProfile
description: Abstraction of credential format complexity behind compliance-oriented identifiers

default_verification_rules.description: DEPRECATED: Use verification_policy_set_id with Cedar policies instead. Legacy opaque verification rules object.

verification_policy_set_id.description: Reference to a PolicySet (policy_type=CREDENTIAL_VERIFICATION) containing Cedar policies that define default verification constraints for this compliance profile (e.g., required formats, holder binding, algorithm restrictions).

trust_profile_constraints.description: Declares which Trust Profile configurations are compatible with this compliance code. Implementations use these constraints to validate that a CredentialTemplate pairs a compatible ComplianceProfile and TrustProfile.

compatible_profile_types.description: The TrustProfile.profile_type values that provide a trust registry suitable for this compliance code. Example: AAMVA_MDL requires ['AAMVA', 'CUSTOM']; EUDI_PID and EUDI_MDL both accept ['EUDI', 'CUSTOM']. CUSTOM is always a valid fallback.

required_source_types.description: TrustSource.source_type values that MUST appear in the linked TrustProfile. Example: ICAO_DTC requires at least one PKD_URL or ROOT_CA source.

required_formats.description: Credential formats that MUST appear in TrustProfile.supported_formats for compatibility.

api_surface.description: Runtime API endpoints that implementations MUST expose when this compliance profile is active. Allows standards-specific APIs (OID4VCI well-known, mDL device engagement) to be declared by the profile rather than hardcoded.

rel.description: IANA link relation or MIP-defined endpoint identifier (e.g., openid-credential-issuer, oauth-authorization-server, mdl-device-engagement)

path_template.description: Path template using {org_id} and {deployment_id} tokens. May be a well-known path.

auth_required.description: Whether this endpoint requires authentication. Wallet-facing endpoints are typically false.

response_schema_ref.description: JSON Schema $id of the expected response schema

standard_ref.description: Standards citation for this endpoint (e.g., OID4VCI §11.2.2, ISO 18013-5 §8.3.1)

discoverable.description: Whether this compliance profile appears in /.well-known/mip-configuration
